paper_title: The Little Ice Age in southern Patagonia: Comparison between paleoecological reconstructions and downscaled model output of a GCM simulation

methods.description: This method involves simulating climate conditions over the last 1000 years using a General Circulation Model (GCM) and applying a statistical downscaling technique to relate large-scale circulation patterns to local precipitation in southeastern Patagonia.

steps.description: Simulate climate conditions over the last 1000 years using a GCM with varying forcing factors (solar, volcanic, greenhouse gases).
steps.searchable_summary: run GCM simulation with historical forcing factors

steps.description: Implement a statistical downscaling technique to relate large-scale circulation of the GCM with local precipitation over South America.
steps.searchable_summary: apply statistical downscaling to GCM output

steps.description: Link the leading five sea level pressure (SLP) principal components of the NCEP-reanalysis over southern South America to precipitation over southeastern Patagonia using multiple linear regression.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple linear regression with SLP principal components

steps.description: Project observed NCEP-EOFs onto the simulated SLP anomaly fields to obtain simulated SLP principal components of the last thousand years.
steps.searchable_summary: project NCEP-EOFs onto simulated SLP fields

steps.description: Use the simulated SLP principal components to reconstruct precipitation over southeastern Patagonia.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct precipitation using simulated SLP components
